Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party David Marrs
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is January 8, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is January 18, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 4, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 1, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than December 15, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by January 2, 2018 at 12:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on January 4, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 22, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is January 5, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 20, 2017. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than October 4, 2017 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than October 4, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on November 15, 2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on January 8, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by January 4, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
